title: 11 Herbal Tinctures For Itchy Eyes
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a great natural remedy for itchy eyes. They work by using the active compounds of herbs to soothe and calm the eyes. For example, Calendula officinalis has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. Aloe barbadensis, on the other hand, is known for its cooling and moisturizing effects, which can provide quick relief from itchy eyes. Hypericum perforatum, or St. John's Wort, has antiviral and antibacterial properties that can help combat infections that may be causing the itchiness.  Herbal tinctures can be made by infusing the herbs in a solvent, such as water or alcohol, and then straining the mixture to create a concentrated liquid. This liquid can be applied directly to the eyes using a cotton ball or swab. The herbs' active compounds are then absorbed through the skin, providing relief from itchy eyes.  Using herbal tinctures for itchy eyes can bring several benefits to your life. For one, they are a natural and chemical-free alternative to over-the-counter eye drops or creams. This makes them a great option for people who are sensitive to chemicals or prefer to avoid them. Additionally, herbal tinctures can be customized to suit individual needs, as different herbs can be combined to create a unique blend. This means that you can create a tincture that is tailored to address specific concerns, such as dryness or redness.  Some common herbs used to make tinctures for itchy eyes include Calendula, Aloe, and Hypericum. These herbs can be purchased in dried or fresh form, and can be combined with other herbs to create a unique blend. When using herbal tinctures, it's essential to follow proper preparation and application instructions to ensure safe and effective use. This includes diluting the tincture with water, as directed, and applying it gently to the eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures contains flavonoids, triterpenoids, and carotenoids, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These bioactive constituents help to reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, making them a good remedy for itchy eyes. The flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have potent antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals and calm the eyes. The triterpenoids, such as oleanolic acid and ursolic acid, exhibit anti-inflammatory activity that reduces redness and itching. By applying Calendula officinalis tinctures to the eyes, the bioactive constituents work together to provide quick relief from itchy and irritated eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal tinctures for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Calendula flowers and 2 cups of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add the Calendula flowers to the vodka and close the jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Label the jar and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 20-30 drops of the tincture in water to soothe itchy eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures contains aloe-emodin, aloin, and barbaloin as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe itchy eyes by reducing redness and swelling. The anthraquinones in aloe vera, like aloe-emodin, also have a calming effect on the eyes, which helps to alleviate itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of aloe vera help to prevent infections that can cause or exacerbate itchy eyes. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, aloe barbadensis tinctures can provide relief from itchy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal tinctures for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather materials: 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, 2 cups of 80% vodka, and a clean glass jar with a lid.
Combine 1 cup of Aloe gel and 2 cups of vodka in the glass jar. Stir well to mix.
Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the Aloe tincture to a dropper bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops in the affected eye 2-3 times a day as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tinctures contains hyperforin and hypericin, which are active constituents that help to reduce inflammation and itching. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, making them effective in calming irritated eyes. Hyperforin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the release of histamine, a chemical that causes itching and allergic reactions. By reducing inflammation and histamine release, Hypericum perforatum tinctures can help to alleviate itchy eyes and provide relief from discomfort. Regular use of the tincture may also help to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing the severity of itchy eye symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal tinctures for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum flowers, 2 cups of 80% ethanol, and a clean glass jar with a lid.
Combine the flowers and ethanol in the jar, making sure the flowers are fully covered.
Stir the mixture well and close the jar. Store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the Hypericum perforatum tincture in a clean glass dropper bottle. Use 5-7 drops in the affected eye 2-3 times a day as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium tinctures contains plant compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. These properties can help reduce itching and swelling in the eyes. The sesquiterpenes, specifically, have been shown to inhibit the release of histamine, a chemical that triggers allergic reactions and itching. The flavonoids in Achillea millefolium have antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the eyes. By reducing inflammation and histamine release, Achillea millefolium tinctures may provide relief from itchy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal tinctures for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers, and 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Combine the Achillea millefolium and vodka in the jar, making sure the plant material is completely covered.
Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the plant material.
Transfer the tincture into smaller dropper bottles and label them. Use 20-30 drops of the tincture in water to help relieve itchy eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphorbia peplus tinctures contains limonoids, such as ingenol and ingenol esters, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, making it an effective remedy for itchy eyes caused by irritation. The anti-inflammatory properties of ingenol and its esters also help to soothe the eyes and calm the surrounding skin, providing relief from discomfort. Additionally, the tannins present in Euphorbia peplus help to reduce the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for itching and inflammation. By reducing histamine release and inflammation, Euphorbia peplus tinctures provide quick and effective relief from itchy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Euphorbia peplus herbal tinctures for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Euphorbia peplus leaves and stems. Wear gloves to protect your skin.
Use a clean blender or coffee grinder to finely chop the Euphorbia peplus.
Transfer the chopped plant material to a clean glass jar. Add 2 cups of 80% vodka or high-proof alcohol.
Close the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis tinctures contains bioactive constituents like ursolic acid, chlorogenic acid, and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling and protect the delicate tissues around the eyes. The tannins in Euphrasia officinalis tinctures also have a soothing effect on the eyes, calming irritation and discomfort. The flavonoids in the tincture have antihistamine properties, which can help alleviate itching and redness caused by allergic reactions or dryness. By reducing inflammation and soothing the eyes, Euphrasia officinalis tinctures can provide relief from itchy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Euphrasia officinalis herbal tinctures for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Euphrasia officinalis flowers and 1 cup of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Fill the jar with the Euphrasia officinalis flowers, leaving about 1 inch of space at the top.
Pour the vodka over the flowers, making sure they are completely covered.
Seal the jar and store it in a dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures contains active constituents like alkylamides, glycosides, and polyphenols. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help to reduce itching and swelling in the eyes. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea tinctures are specifically known for their ability to inhibit the release of histamine, a chemical that can cause itching and inflammation. The glycosides in the tincture also have soothing and protective effects on the eyes, helping to calm irritation and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and inhibiting histamine release, Echinacea purpurea tinctures can provide relief from itchy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Echinacea purpurea flowers and 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Fill the jar 2/3 with the Echinacea flowers. Leave 1/3 of the jar empty.
Pour the vodka over the Echinacea flowers, making sure they are completely covered.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the tincture extract.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle. Discard the Echinacea flowers and store the tincture in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale tinctures contains the bioactive constituents allantoin, rosmarinic acid, and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm itchy eyes. Allantoin, in particular, is known to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mucous membranes, making it an effective treatment for itchy eyes. Rosmarinic acid has potent antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation, further contributing to the relief of itchy eyes. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Symphytum officinale tinctures can provide quick and effective relief from itchy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal tinctures for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Symphytum officinale leaves and flowers. Clean them with water.
Fill a clean glass jar with the Symphytum officinale leaves and flowers. Leave 1 inch of space at the top.
Cover the leaves and flowers with 2 cups of 80% ethanol (vodka or rum work too) in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids. Use the liquid as a tincture for itchy eyes. Start with a few drops and see how you react.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata tinctures contains iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm irritated eyes. The flavonoids, particularly aucubin and catalpol, have been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory effects that reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. The phenolic acids, such as chlorogenic acid, also have antioxidant properties that help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage. By reducing inflammation and protecting the eyes from damage, Plantago lanceolata tinctures can help to alleviate itchy eyes and promote overall eye health.
remedies.image_alt: Plantago lanceolata herbal tinctures for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Plantago lanceolata leaves and flowers. Clean and dry them with a paper towel.
Combine the dried Plantago lanceolata with 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar. The leaves and flowers should be completely covered.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Label the jar with the date and contents, and store it in a cool, dark place. To use, put 20-30 drops of the tincture in a cup of water and use as an eye wash for itchy eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum tinctures contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The flavonoids, particularly silymarin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help soothe itchy eyes. Saponins, specifically silybin, have been shown to inhibit the production of histamine, a chemical that triggers allergic reactions and itching. The anti-inflammatory properties of Silybum marianum tinctures help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from itchy eyes caused by allergies or irritants. By reducing inflammation and inhibiting histamine production, Silybum marianum tinctures can help calm itchy eyes and promote a sense of comfort.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal tinctures for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 250ml of 80% ethanol, Silybum marianum flowers, and a clean glass jar.
Combine 50g of Silybum marianum flowers with 250ml of 80% ethanol in the glass jar.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Label the jar with the date and the name of the tincture, and store it in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tinctures contains the bioactive constituents histamine, serotonin, and isothiocyanates. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. Histamine, in particular, is known to have a natural antihistamine effect, which can counteract the release of histamine by the body in response to allergens. Serotonin and isothiocyanates have antioxidant properties that can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and protecting the eyes, Urtica dioica tinctures can help alleviate itchy eyes and provide relief from allergic reactions.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal tinctures for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves and flowers. Wear gloves when handling to avoid skin irritation.
Combine the Urtica dioica leaves and flowers in a clean glass jar. Leave about 1 inch of space at the top.
Pour 2 cups of vodka over the Urtica dioica mixture. Make sure the leaves and flowers are completely covered.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar every day to help the tincture extract.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle. Discard the solids and use 5-10 drops of the tincture in water for itchy eyes.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea, especially one with anti-inflammatory properties like chamomile or calendula, may help soothe itchy eyes. These teas can reduce redness and swelling, making them a potential remedy for itchy eyes. They work by calming the skin and reducing irritation, which can provide relief from itchy eyes.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for itchy eyes every day is generally safe, but some people may experience issues. For instance, if you have allergies, certain herbs could make your symptoms worse. Choose teas with soothing properties like chamomile or calendula, and be aware of how your body reacts.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with itchy eyes, but it may take some time to see results. Some people experience relief within a few days, while others may take up to two weeks to notice a difference. The effectiveness can vary depending on the type of tea and individual tolerance.
faq_time: For itchy eyes, try drinking herbal tea in the evening, after dinner. This allows the soothing ingredients to take effect as you prepare for bed. The calmness can help reduce eye irritation and promote a restful night's sleep.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, including those for itchy eyes. For example, if you're taking a medication for dry eyes and also drinking tea that contains caffeine or ginkgo biloba, it might affect how the medication works. This could make your symptoms worse or change how you feel.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and have itchy eyes, some herbal teas might be tempting. However, it's best to be cautious with what you drink. Some herbs like chamomile and peppermint are generally safe, but others like pennyroyal and red clover may not be suitable for pregnant women. Always read the labels and ask questions if unsure.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for itchy eyes can be a relief, but when breastfeeding, it's best to be cautious. Some herbs, like chamomile and peppermint, are generally considered safe, but others, like eye drops herbs like eucalyptus and menthol, might pass into breast milk. It's a good idea to choose teas with ingredients known to be safe during breastfeeding.
title_2: 11 Herbal Tinctures to Give Asthmatics Relief from Itchy Eyes
